nodes.name: KalmanFilterInit
nodes.description: Bootstraps the Kalman filter state by initialising the error covariance matrix P, process-noise covariance Q, measurement-noise covariance R, and the prior state estimate X. No computation is performed; all values are stored as immutable starting state for downstream kernels.

inputs.name: estimated_measurement_variance
inputs.type_desc: float — initial error covariance estimate (P) and prior state estimate (X)

nodes.type_signature: (process_variance: float — diagonal process-noise variance (Q), measurement_variance: float — diagonal measurement-noise variance (R), estimated_measurement_variance: float — initial error covariance estimate (P) and prior state estimate (X)) -> tuple[float — initial error covariance, float — process-noise covariance, float — measurement-noise covariance, float — prior state estimate]
nodes.conceptual_summary: RecursiveEstimatorHyperparameterSeedInitializer. Establishes the immutable prior probability scaffold for a Bayesian recursive estimator by encoding four scalar uncertainty parameters into a fixed initial state manifold. It partitions total system uncertainty into two orthogonal noise sources — latent-state transition noise and observation noise — alongside an initial belief covariance and a prior latent-state estimate, creating a complete probabilistic starting configuration. No algebraic transformation occurs; the operation is a pure structured assignment that seeds the downstream iterative update kernel with a well-defined prior distribution.. Applications: Robotics & autonomous navigation: seeding the prior belief state of a localization filter that fuses inertial odometry (high process noise) with sparse GPS fixes (high measurement noise) to track a mobile agent's position over time., Computational neuroscience: initializing the prior parameters of a Bayesian brain model that infers latent neural firing rates from noisy electrophysiological recordings, where process noise captures neural state variability and measurement noise captures electrode noise., Climate & geophysical modeling: bootstrapping a sequential data-assimilation system that blends a numerical weather prediction model (process noise = model uncertainty) with sparse satellite observations (measurement noise = instrument error) to estimate atmospheric state variables., Quantitative signal processing / IoT sensor fusion: configuring the initial uncertainty hyperparameters for a recursive smoothing algorithm that tracks a slowly drifting physical quantity (e.g., temperature, pressure, altitude) from a noisy low-cost MEMS sensor stream.

nodes.name: KalmanMeasurementUpdate
nodes.description: Pure functional Kalman predict-and-update kernel. Consumes the current immutable state tuple (P, Q, R, X) together with a scalar measurement and produces a brand-new state tuple (P_new, X_new). Internally fuses the time-update (predict) step — advancing P by Q — with the measurement-update step that computes the Kalman gain, corrects the state estimate X, and contracts P by the complement of the gain. Returns new objects; the input state is never mutated.
nodes.type_signature: (P: float — error covariance (state_in), Q: float — process-noise covariance (static), R: float — measurement-noise covariance (static), X: float — prior state estimate (state_in), measurement: float — scalar observation) -> tuple[float — updated error covariance (state_out), float — posterior state estimate (state_out)]
nodes.conceptual_summary: ScalarGaussianPriorObservationFuser. Accepts a prior scalar estimate and its associated uncertainty (covariance), advances the uncertainty forward in time by additive process-noise, then computes an optimal convex-combination weight (the gain) from the ratio of prior uncertainty to total uncertainty (prior + observation noise). Uses that gain to blend the prior estimate toward the scalar observation and to contract the uncertainty by the complement of the gain — yielding a posterior estimate that is the minimum-variance unbiased fusion of two competing Gaussian beliefs. The entire operation is a pure function: all inputs are read-only and a new (posterior-estimate, posterior-uncertainty) pair is returned.. Applications: Geophysical sensor fusion: continuously refining an estimate of subsurface temperature or pressure at a fixed depth by blending a slow-drift model (Q) with noisy point-sensor readings (R), producing a running posterior depth-state used by autonomous drilling controllers., Epidemiological prevalence tracking: maintaining a running scalar estimate of the true infection rate in a population where each new aggregate test-batch yields a noisy count; Q captures day-to-day biological drift and R captures sampling variability, producing a posterior prevalence used to trigger intervention thresholds., Adaptive signal denoising in telecommunications: estimating the true amplitude of a slowly varying carrier envelope from a stream of noisy sampled observations, where Q models the envelope's random walk and R models receiver noise, enabling real-time gain-control with minimal lag., Robotic proprioception calibration: tracking the latent scalar bias-drift of an inertial measurement unit (e.g., gyroscope zero-offset) by fusing a Brownian-motion drift model with intermittent zero-velocity calibration pulses, producing a continuously corrected bias estimate that feeds downstream attitude computation.
